skill.name: finishing-branches
skill.description: Guides final branch checks, pull request preparation, and cleanup after work is ready for integration.
skill.summary: Finish development branches with checks, review steps, pull request creation, and post-merge cleanup.
skill.author: BPSAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many external command patterns and weak cryptography matches. The command patterns are real shell examples for tests, git review, PR creation, and branch cleanup, so they require user review before execution. The weak cryptography matches are false positives caused by ordinary words such as description and Brief description, with no cryptographic operation present.

medium_findings.title: Repository-Changing Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends shell commands that can create pull requests, change branches, delete local branches, stage files, commit, and push. These commands match the branch finishing purpose, but users should review the branch state and command arguments before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly visible in fenced bash blocks and include git and PR operations. They appear legitimate for this skill, but they can modify repository state if run without review.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts point to descriptive text, not crypto code. Lines 3, 44, and 73 contain ordinary description wording, and no weak algorithm use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines do not contain cryptographic code or configuration. The match appears to be substring-based on normal prose.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash command examples for CI, validation, secret scanning, git review, PR creation, and branch cleanup. These are not automatically executed by the skill, but they should be treated as commands requiring user approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence consists of explicit fenced bash command blocks. No hidden execution or obfuscation was found, but the pattern is relevant because the commands can affect local git state.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss final checks before merging completed branch work. This skill gives a concise finish workflow for validation, review, pull request creation, and cleanup.
actual_capabilities: Lists pre-merge checks for CI, validation, and staged secret scanning.
Provides git diff commands for reviewing branch changes against main.
Supplies a pull request template with summary, changes, testing, and checklist sections.
Shows post-merge cleanup commands for returning to main and deleting a local branch.
Includes a quick finish command sequence for test, lint, commit, and push workflows.
limitations: Assumes the project uses bpsai-pair commands for CI, validation, security scanning, and PR creation.
Does not inspect repository status or verify that commands are safe for the current branch.
Does not create pull requests by itself; it provides command guidance.
Does not replace project-specific release, approval, or compliance procedures.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature Branch for Review
use_cases.description: Run final checks, inspect diffs, and create a structured pull request before asking for review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Merge Readiness
use_cases.description: Give contributors a repeatable checklist for tests, validation, secret scanning, and task status updates.

use_cases.title: Clean Up After Integration
use_cases.description: Return to the main branch, pull the latest changes, and remove the completed local branch.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Finish Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-branches skill to help me prepare my completed branch for a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Review Before PR Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-branches skill to guide final checks and show what I should review before creating a PR.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer wants a careful pre-merge review

prompt_templates.title: Create a PR Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-branches skill to draft a pull request summary from my completed changes and testing notes.

prompt_templates.title: Finish and Clean Up Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-branches skill to plan the final validation, PR creation, and post-merge branch cleanup steps.

output_examples.input: I finished a feature branch and need to open a PR.
output_examples.output: The assistant provides a pre-merge checklist, suggests reviewing the branch diff, and drafts a PR body with testing and checklist sections.

output_examples.input: My PR merged. What should I do next?
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends returning to the main branch, updating it from origin, and deleting the completed local branch after confirming the merge.

output_examples.input: Help me make sure I did not leave debug work in my branch.
output_examples.output: The assistant points to diff review and debug keyword checks, then reminds the user to update task status and verify tests.

best_practices: Review every command before running it because several commands modify git state.
Run tests, linting, validation, and secret scanning before creating a pull request.
Update task status and documentation before marking branch work complete.
anti_patterns: Do not run the quick finish sequence without checking the staged changes first.
Do not delete a branch until the merge is confirmed and no local work remains.
Do not use the provided PR template as a substitute for required project approvals.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides command examples and workflow guidance that users must review before execution.

faq.question: Which repositories does it work best with?
faq.answer: It works best with git repositories that use bpsai-pair tooling and pull request based integration.

faq.question: Can it create a pull request?
faq.answer: It shows a command for automated PR creation through bpsai-pair, but the user controls execution.

faq.question: Does it check for leaked secrets?
faq.answer: It recommends a staged secret scan command as part of the pre-merge checklist.

faq.question: Is it safe for new contributors?
faq.answer: Yes, if contributors review commands first and confirm the current branch and staged files.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill contains legitimate shell commands that can change repository state, so users need a clear execution warning.
